title: pluchea paniculata var. gardneri
intro: Pluchea paniculata var. gardneri, also known as Bushy White-flower or Bushy White Top, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits associated with Pluchea paniculata var. gardneri include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
